vocabulary.title: Soda Data Quality Platform Vocabulary
vocabulary.description: Key terms and concepts used in the Soda Cloud REST API and data quality platform.

terms.definition: A data quality rule applied to a dataset column or table. Checks evaluate conditions such as row counts, missing values, duplicates, or custom SQL expressions and produce a pass/fail/warn/error result.
Soda Checks Language — the YAML-based domain-specific language used to define data quality checks. SodaCL checks are evaluated during scans.
The execution of data quality checks against a datasource. A scan runs the configured SodaCL checks and produces check results, incidents, and notifications.
A named configuration that specifies which checks to run and on which schedule. Scan definitions are stored in Soda Cloud and can be triggered programmatically.
A table, view, or other data object within a datasource that is registered in Soda Cloud for monitoring. Datasets have associated checks, attributes, and quality scores.
A connection to a data warehouse, database, or data lake (e.g., Snowflake, BigQuery, Databricks, PostgreSQL, DuckDB). Datasources contain datasets that can be monitored.
A data quality issue automatically created when checks fail. Incidents have severity levels (minor, major, critical) and statuses (reported, investigating, fixing, resolved).
A formal specification defining the expected structure, quality standards, and SLAs for a dataset. Contracts can be created manually, as skeletons, or AI-generated, then published and verified.
An agent process that executes scan workloads. Runners can be self-hosted (deployed in your infrastructure) or Soda-hosted (managed cloud runners).
A custom metadata field that can be attached to datasets, checks, or columns. Attribute types include text, number, checkbox, datetime, singleSelect, and multiSelect.
A configuration that defines when and how alerts are sent when check or monitor results meet certain conditions. Rules can filter by severity, dataset, or check status.
An encrypted credential stored in Soda Cloud, used for datasource connections. Secrets are encrypted client-side using a JWK public key before being stored.
A table or view found in a datasource during discovery scans but not yet onboarded into Soda Cloud for active monitoring.
The process of registering a discovered dataset into Soda Cloud for monitoring, check definition, and quality tracking.
An automated monitor that tracks statistical metrics (e.g., mean, standard deviation, null rate) for a specific column over time and generates alerts when anomalies are detected.
A monitor based on a user-defined SQL query that computes a metric value, which is then tracked for anomalies or threshold breaches.
The authentication mechanism used by the Soda Cloud REST API. Credentials are base64-encoded in the format api_key_id:api_key_secret and passed in the Authorization header.
A pair of credentials (API Key ID and API Key Secret) used to authenticate with the Soda Cloud REST API. Keys are managed in Soda Cloud profile settings.
A data quality dimension that measures how recently a dataset was updated. Soda can monitor freshness by checking the maximum value of timestamp columns.
The result of a check evaluation: pass (condition met), fail (condition violated), warn (warning threshold crossed), error (check execution error), or unknown (could not evaluate).
